untranslatable_reason: This is a meta-rule for validating trading rule symmetry and directional bias, not a direct market entry/exit signal. It requires: (1) comparison of rule trigger counts between long/short conditions (not available as indicators), (2) backtest performance metrics across regimes (out-of-sample statistical analysis), and (3) regime cycle counting (requires external backtest framework). The core validation—that entry condition symmetry must be within ±5% and performance divergence <10% across opposing trends—cannot be expressed as real-time candlestick conditions.
translation_notes: ARONSON_DRY_0_0 is a regime validation filter for rule robustness testing, not a tradeable signal. Implement this as a backtest-time rule validator: (A) Count rule trigger occurrences in TRENDING_UP vs TRENDING_DOWN regimes—reject if long/short counts differ >5%; (B) Calculate Sharpe/win% separately for up-trend and down-trend subsets—reject if performance delta >10%; (C) Require minimum 3 complete market cycles in historical data. This should be applied as a gating condition before accepting any rule into production.
